extra.reasoning: Property: never (rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b). Only r6 sets rec_drug_a, and only when the full 10-way conjunction (peptic_ulcer & ckd_dialysis & elderly & asthma & bradycardia & prior_gi_bleed & seizure_history & immunosuppressed & hyperkalemia & pregnancy) is true; otherwise rec_drug_a defaults to false. For rec_drug_b, r5 (priority 50, peptic_ulcer -> true) and r2 (priority 90, same 10-way conjunction -> false) are the relevant rules. Whenever rec_drug_a can be true (the 10-way conjunction holds), r2's condition is identically satisfied, and r2 has priority 90 > r5's priority 50, so r2 wins and forces rec_drug_b := false. Thus whenever rec_drug_a=true, rec_drug_b is necessarily false. In all other inputs rec_drug_a is false (default). The remaining inputs (diabetes, anticoagulated, hepatic_impairment) only affect rec_drug_c and are irrelevant. Therefore rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b can never be jointly true; the mutual-exclusion property holds over the entire input space. The high-priority r2 was clearly designed to suppress drug_b exactly in the case where drug_a fires.
